Ad Text:
Microsoft Fabric Data Engineer 

Excellent opportunity

Work from home 4 days a week and Wednesdays in our Ashburton office

Immediate start

Azured are a Microsoft Partner delivering professional and managed services to organisations throughout Australia, ranging from small and medium size businesses through to large enterprises with national and global operations.

Azured is growing our Data & AI capability, and we’re looking for a Microsoft Fabric Data Engineer who loves designing and building modern cloud data solutions, and solving real business problems for customers.

What will you be doing in this role?

Designing and building Microsoft Fabric solutions around medallion architectures

Working directly with customers to understand requirements and shape solutions

Developing data pipelines, lakehouses, and warehouses

Developing transformation logic using Notebooks (PySpark), SQL, and Dataflows Gen2

Creating and optimising semantic models for reporting teams

Leading customers through data governance uplifts with Purview Unified Catalog

Collaborating with senior engineers and architects across Azure, AI, and modern data platforms

How do you get shortlisted for this role?

2 years hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric

3 years experience with Delta Lake

3 years experience in data modelling (star schema, dimensional modelling)

2 years experience with Notebook development on Fabric, Synapse or Databricks

2 years experience using Fabric Data Pipelines, Azured Data Factory, Dataflows Gen2

4 years SQL development experience on Microsoft SQL Server or Azure SQL Database

Experience building and maintaining semantic models

Strong communication skills

Nice to have

Purview or Fabric governance

Azure Synapse, ADF, Databricks, Azure SQL

Experience in professional services, MSP, or consulting environments

Apply now!

Lab3 is one of the largest Azure engineering practices in the Australasia region with a focus on Cloud & DevOps, Data, IoT & AI, Modern Workplace, Modern Apps & Integration, Security, and Network services.

We are on the hunt for a Senior Data Engineer with experience in Azure Data Engineering & ideally Fabric for a 3-month contract (extendable

You must be in NZ and have resided here for 5 years+ to be considered for the role (to enable government security clearance).

The role:

Manage, support, and deliver the engineering of state-of-the-art technical solutions on Azure Data Platforms
Lead the hands-on end-to-end planning, provisioning, and implementation of Azure Data services 
Provide infrastructure and automation expertise to uplift, deliver and deploy Data Warehousing, Data Functions and end-to-end data analytics solutions.  
Design solutions using Azure Data Factory, Azure Synapse, Azure Data Warehousing, Data Lakes, and App Insights.  
Work with business stakeholders to help gather specifications and deliver effective solutions based on an understanding of data domains and technology.
Support data warehousing including manipulation, migration & integration.
Provision, modify, support, and manage complex Microsoft PaaS SQL Server databases as well as high availability environments that include SQL clusters, Always on and Availability Groups. Migrate on-prem services into Azure cloud platforms.  
Log using Log Analytics Workspace & configuration of diagnostic settings. 
Support in-house and external engineers with data platform configuration used for data ingestion, manipulation, extraction, and cleansing. 

The right candidate:

Significant design and & delivery experience in Azure Data Engineering, ideally including Fabric
Ability to understand business requirements and design effective solutions using deep Azure data engineering skills.
Expertise in the Azure Data Platform - Microsoft frameworks (Cloud-based analytics), Data Catalog, Data Lineage, ML
Extensive experience in Azure Data, Azure Data Factory, Azure Data Warehousing, Azure Synapse, Azure Databricks, data modelling , Azure SQL, Big Data, Data Lakes and App Insights and Fabric.
Extensive experience in conventional Microsoft BI technologies such as SSIS | SSRS | SSAS
Expertise in database concepts: SQL Server, Azure SQL DB/MI and Application Services configuration  
Azure DevOps & Automation, DevOps Integration, DataOps experience
Knowledge of broader Azure Resources, CI/CD,Azure DevOps, IaC, ETL, CosmosDB, Event Hub, Scorecards, Reporting & Dashboards, Service Oriented Architectures (SOA) and programming 
T-SQL, Hadoop, Spark, Kafka, Python, Scala, Impala, R, Power BI - desirable 
New Zealand citizen or permanent resident with 5+ years in NZ, thereby eligible for government security clearance
You should be available within 2 weeks for a 3-month contract on a daily rate
Based anywhere in New Zealand

What's in it for you?

Become part of Australasia's largest Azure engineering practice where you can sharpen your expertise and contribute to the latest innovations in tech and services. #thinkahead
Be part of a company that is leading the way in specialised areas and partners on high profile client projects - we are popping new logos every day! #winwithus
Work with a 13-time Gold, 6-time Microsoft Advanced Specialisation & 5 time Microsoft Solutions Partner (including Modern Work). You only get to this level with the industry's leading tech professionals - we are proud to have some of the best. #teamup
Thrive in a team that is always learning, always innovating and making waves on a global scale. #standout
Be part of a supportive working environment that values inclusion, flexibility, diversity and differences. #betrue
Work flexibly – we recognise the importance of your overall wellbeing! #Flexit!
Ultimately, we will help you to fear less, achieve more in a fast paced, innovative and cool environment - are you ready to accept the challenge? #fearlessachievemore

If you have questions mail me on gayatri.soni@lab3nz.co.nz or call 0212379092. Otherwise apply now!
